businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla extends beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxis, Optimus humanoid robots, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue was $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08. Q4 2025 gross margin reached 20.1%, a two-year high. FSD has shifted to a $99/month subscription model, and April 2026 brings Cybercab production and Roadster unveiling as key catalysts.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme premium vs S&P 500 avg ~23x and auto sector avg ~12x. Down from 368x but still extraordinarily high

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on 2026E EPS $2.02. Over 4x the growth stock average of 30-40x. Prices in robotaxi, energy, and robot success

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 revenue $94.8B grew just 1% YoY. 2026 expected at $108.9B (+15%)

keyMetrics.name: Q1 EV Market Share
keyMetrics.interpretation: US EV sales plunged 28% in Q1 but Tesla's share recovered above 50%, earning 'big winner' status. 3.3% of total auto market

keyMetrics.name: RSI
keyMetrics.interpretation: Approaching oversold territory (below 30). Dropped sharply from 37.49 in one day. Technical bounce increasingly likely

keyMetrics.name: Next Earnings
keyMetrics.interpretation: Q1 delivery consensus at 365,645 units. Earnings and Cybercab update will determine near-term direction

growthDrivers.title: Tesla Named 'Big Winner' in 28% EV Sales Slump (3/27)
growthDrivers.description: Benzinga reported that US Q1 EV sales dropped 28% but Tesla's market share recovered above 50%. High gas prices ($112/barrel) are driving increased EV search interest

growthDrivers.title: Oil at $112, Accelerating EV Transition
growthDrivers.description: Iran war escalation pushed Brent crude to $112. Rising gas prices maximize EV economics and consumer switching incentives

growthDrivers.title: Optimus3 Robot Video Released, Summer Production (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Tesla revealed Optimus3 humanoid robot design with reduction gears and precision hands. Target: 1M production rate by end of 2026

growthDrivers.title: April Catalysts: Cybercab Production + Roadster Unveiling
growthDrivers.description: Cybercab mass production and next-gen Roadster unveiling both scheduled for April, concentrating two major catalysts

growthDrivers.title: Netherlands FSD Approval Expected 4/10
growthDrivers.description: FSD Supervised approval expected by April 10. If approved, EU-wide rollout possible by summer

growthDrivers.title: Terafab $25B AI Chip Factory
growthDrivers.description: Wedbush called the Terafab semiconductor plant a 'game changer,' maintaining $600 target

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share, 8.4B miles of FSD data, Supercharger NACS standard dominance, Terafab AI chip vertical integration

moats.description: 8.4 billion miles of real-world FSD driving data. Impossible for competitors to replicate in the short term
Supercharger network adopted as NACS standard. Even Ford and GM use Tesla's charging infrastructure
Battery cells, AI chips (Terafab), software, charging infrastructure all in-house
Synonymous with EVs. High brand recognition drives demand without marketing spend

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: 4.54M vehicles sold in 2025, world's largest by volume. But China Feb sales collapsed 65%

competitors.name: Zoox (Amazon)
competitors.detail: Expanding robotaxi service in SF and Las Vegas. Direct robotaxi competitor

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Ahead of Tesla in autonomous commercialization. 200K+ paid rides per week

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: Growing global EV share with Ioniq and EV6 lineups

recentNews.headline: TSLA -2.76%, $361.83 Close, RSI 33.86 Enters Oversold Zone
recentNews.significance: Fifth straight weekly decline. Dow entered correction territory (-10% from peak). TSLA RSI hit 33.86, approaching oversold

recentNews.headline: Benzinga: Tesla 'Big Winner' in Brutal 28% EV Sales Slump
recentNews.significance: US Q1 EV sales dropped 28% but Tesla reclaimed 50%+ EV market share. EV search volume surging on high gas prices

recentNews.headline: Gene Munster: 'Tesla Delivery Data Could Be Market Catalyst'
recentNews.significance: Deepwater Asset Management's Munster told CNBC that Tesla Q1 delivery data could catalyze a broader market rebound

recentNews.headline: Dow Plunges 793 Points, Enters Correction, 5th Weekly Loss
recentNews.significance: Iran war pushes oil to $112, VIX to 31.05 (+13%). Dow, Nasdaq, S&P all down for 5th straight week

recentNews.headline: Q1 2026 Delivery Consensus: 365,645 Vehicles
recentNews.significance: 23-analyst consensus. +8% YoY from Q1 2025 but -13% from Q4 2025

analystOpinions.notableComment: 31-analyst average $399. Wedbush $600 high, Needham $25 low. Upside expanded to 10.3% on price decline. Gene Munster sees deliveries as potential catalyst

risks.title: RSI 33.86 Oversold + 5 Straight Weekly Losses
risks.description: RSI approaching oversold territory. Below both 50-day ($409) and 200-day ($396) SMAs. Selling momentum self-reinforcing

risks.title: Extreme Valuation vs Growth Stagnation
risks.description: P/E 357.6x while revenue growth is just +1%. Only justified if robotaxi, energy, and robot ambitions all succeed

risks.title: Market-Wide Panic, Dow in Correction
risks.description: Dow -793pts into correction. VIX 31.05, oil $112. Risk-off sentiment at extreme levels

risks.title: Q1 EV Market Down 28%
risks.description: US Q1 new EV sales plunged 28%. Tesla held share but absolute volume down 4.6% YoY

risks.title: FSD Regulatory Risk
risks.description: NHTSA investigation escalated, California robotaxi application denied. Trefis warns '$1T AI thesis could break'

risks.title: Musk Risk (Brand Damage)
risks.description: DOGE political activity causing European sales collapse. Insider selling. Brand damage impacting demand

buyReasons.title: RSI 33.86 Oversold, Technical Bounce Imminent
buyReasons.rationale: RSI below 35 historically precedes rebounds. 27% below 52-week high

buyReasons.title: EV Market 'Big Winner' with 50%+ Share
buyReasons.rationale: Despite 28% market decline, Tesla dominates. High oil prices accelerating EV adoption

buyReasons.title: April Catalyst Concentration
buyReasons.rationale: Cybercab, Roadster, Netherlands FSD, Q1 earnings (4/21) all in April

buyReasons.title: Oil at $112 Maximizes EV Economics
buyReasons.rationale: Highest oil prices make EV cost advantage more compelling than ever

overallOpinion: RSI hit 33.86, entering oversold territory. Five straight weekly declines with the Dow entering correction. Market-wide panic with VIX at 31.05 and oil at $112
Yet Tesla was named 'big winner' in the 28% EV sales slump, reclaiming 50%+ market share. High oil prices are driving renewed EV search interest and adoption
April brings concentrated catalysts: Cybercab production, Roadster unveiling, FSD Europe approval, and Q1 earnings (4/21). Gene Munster sees delivery data as a potential market catalyst
Closed at $361.83. Below both 50-day ($409) and 200-day ($396) SMAs. RSI signals technical bounce potential, but market panic hasn't subsided. Wait for 4/21 earnings

investmentVerdict.summary: RSI oversold and 'big winner' EV status support a bounce, but Dow correction and VIX 31 suggest the bottom isn't confirmed yet. Hold for existing holders, wait for 4/21 earnings before new entry

sources.name: Benzinga/247WallSt - EV Sales
sources.description: Q1 US EV sales -28%, Tesla 50%+ share

sources.description: Tesla Q1 122,196 units (-4.6% YoY), 3.3% total market share

sources.description: Dow enters correction, Gene Munster interview

sources.name: Trefis - $1T AI Thesis
sources.description: Physical AI pivot analysis, FSD investigation

threads.hook: TSLA: RSI 33 oversold, the only EV winner in a crashing market
threads.text:
$TSLA $361.83(-2.76%), market cap $1.36T

RSI hit 33.86, entering oversold. Fifth straight weekly decline as the Dow enters correction territory.

But in the 28% EV sales slump, Tesla reclaimed 50%+ market share. Benzinga called it 'the big winner.' Oil at $112 is boosting EV demand.

April catalysts concentrated: Cybercab, Roadster, FSD Europe, Q1 earnings (4/21). Gene Munster: 'delivery data could catalyze the market.'

Hold. RSI is oversold but market panic hasn't ended. April 21 is the inflection point.

witty.text:

EV sales dropped 28%. Tesla's share went UP to 50%+. 'Big winner,' says Benzinga. Yet the stock fell for the 5th straight week.

When the market panics, even good news can't save you

April brings Cybercab, Roadster, and earnings. Proof or bust

Historically, bounces happen here.

But market panics can push RSI into the 20s.
Hold. Eyes on April 21

witty.hook: EV market crashed 28%, Tesla's the only winner, but the stock keeps falling
witty.text:
$TSLA $361.83(-2.76%)

EV sales plunged 28% but Tesla's share went UP. Benzinga called it 'the big winner.' Yet the stock is down for the 5th straight week. Market logic.

Dow entered correction. VIX 31. Oil $112. Even good news can't fight macro panic. P/E 358x doesn't help either.

RSI 33.86. Oversold doorstep. April Cybercab, Roadster, earnings could flip the script.

Target $399 (+10%). Hold. The market will remember Tesla when the panic ends.

empathy.hook: TSLA shareholders, five straight weekly losses must be exhausting
empathy.text:
$TSLA $361.83(-2.76%), 27% off the high.

Five straight weeks of decline. The Dow's in correction too. There's nowhere to hide right now.

But Tesla emerged as 'the big winner' in the 28% EV crash. Oil at $112 is pushing EV demand higher. Gene Munster sees delivery data as a catalyst.

RSI 33.86 means we're near oversold. Historically, bounces start here. April brings Cybercab, Roadster, and earnings.

Don't panic. April 21 will show the way.
